changes_made: Iteration 1: Rewrote skill to force Python script execution — model got stuck calling 'question' tool in loop (0/0)
Iteration 2: Switched to csv module (no pandas) to avoid dtype errors — same question tool loop (0/0)
Iteration 3: Restored original skill as baseline verification — high variance (77/2)
Iteration 4: Added Quick Reference checklist with conversion examples, JSON format guidance, missing row rules to original skill (91/20)

Iteration 5: Expanded examples for all analytes (calcium, cholesterol, glucose 9.5), added empty cell detection patterns (,,), explicit JSON template with closing braces, total_rows counting rule (71/23)
observations: qwen3-30b has extremely high run-to-run variance on opencode (task 1 ranged 0-100, task 2 ranged 0-23)
Forcing the model to write and run Python scripts caused it to fall into a 'question' tool loop and produce no output
The model works best when computing conversions in-head and writing output directly via the write tool
Task 1 (3 analytes, 20 rows) is manageable for in-head computation; task 2 (8 analytes, 30 rows) exceeds the model's reliable capacity
Persistent task 2 failures: invalid JSON (missing closing brace), wrong total_rows (counts header), wrong excluded_rows, includes missing rows in cleaned output
Adding more conversion examples improved values-in-range (0→0.75→0.875) but structural output issues remained
